orders.note: duplicate lot — same day as filled AUBANK 50 @ 1050
duplicate lot — same day as filled NESTLEIND 35 @ 1410
3rd AUBANK lot — stacks on 50 filled + 70 open

findings: Filled-book state is within all current overrides: deployed 51.96% < 0.75 cap; largest single name MAHABANK at 10.53% < 0.15 cap; no realized PnL today (market not yet open at 09:00).

BFSI concentration on filled book is the dominant sector exposure: MAHABANK + PNBHOUSING + AUBANK = ₹229,020 = 24.65% of total capital and 47.42% of the deployed book. Still within tolerance but the single largest cluster by a wide margin — a BFSI-systemic headline (rate surprise, PSU-bank news) is the primary single-factor drawdown risk for today.

OPEN-order overhang carried from 2026-04-22 is the key carry-forward risk: 5 orders totaling ₹302,665 (32.57% of capital) are pending. On full fill, deployed jumps to 84.54% (breaches 0.75 cap) and AUBANK single-name reaches 19.87% (breaches 0.15 cap). These OPEN orders are grandfathered under the pre-override acceptance pattern established yesterday, so tighter caps applied now will NOT cancel them — they only gate new entries.

Duplicate-order hygiene is the structural issue behind the AUBANK cap-breach risk: three AUBANK lots (70, 56, 50) and two NESTLEIND lots (50, 35) were emitted within the same session on 2026-04-22. Strategy layer's EOD note added ORDER HYGIENE to the system prompt to address this at the decision layer; expect the strategy agent to cancel/reshape duplicates at market open before they fill cumulatively.

Emerging Power cluster: TORNTPOWER (filled) + NTPC (open) + ADANIPOWER (open) → ₹159,183 = 17.13% of capital if all fill. Second-largest cluster on full-fill scenario; warrants attention but not yet a breach target (no Power-specific override exists).
Emerging FMCG cluster: NESTLEIND full (after open fill) + HINDUNILVR = ₹167,540 = 18.03% of capital if NESTLEIND open fills. Comparable magnitude to Power cluster.

Realized PnL telemetry is still zero (no sells executed in Day 1; no trades yet today). Daily-loss gate (0.03) is not a live risk vector this tick — same rationale as yesterday's 14:30 / 16:00 decisions.
No intraday positions (paper_positions table empty) — entire exposure is in CNC holdings, so intraday-leverage risk is not a concern.
actions_taken: No numeric override changes this tick. Holding min_confidence=0.60, max_position_pct=0.15, max_deployed_pct=0.75 into market open.

Rationale: at 09:00 premarket there is no new realized-outcome data to react to (market not yet open; holdings unchanged from 2026-04-22 close; zero trades today). The changelog pattern has been to add one lever per observable risk inflection; the OPEN-book overhang is a known, already-priced-in concern from yesterday's 14:30 0.75 deployed-cap override, and further tightening would not retract grandfathered OPEN orders. The ORDER HYGIENE / CLUSTER DISCIPLINE prompt additions from yesterday's 16:00 EOD are the intended Day-1→Day-2 handoff mechanism for the duplicate AUBANK/NESTLEIND problem — let the strategy layer observe the open and act (cancel duplicates, trim stacked lots) before adding more numeric friction.

Explicit DID-NOT-TIGHTEN reasoning: (a) daily_loss_limit_pct held at 0.03 — realized PnL is ₹0, no live loss vector; (b) min_risk_reward held at 1.5 — R:R quality has not been identified as the live driver of Day-1 underperformance (per 14:30 / 15:00 / 16:00 notes); (c) min_confidence held at 0.60 — on Day-1 sample, 0.72-confidence trades (MAHABANK, NESTLEIND) were among the larger mark-to-market losers, so raising the floor is not yet an evidence-backed lever; (d) default_sl_pct / drawdown_* held — no drawdown observed, SL-tightening without realized-loss data would be premature.

Reassessment trigger for next tick: if market opens and (i) OPEN-book fills push AUBANK past 0.15 despite grandfathering, evaluate whether a one-time position-trim directive is warranted; (ii) deployed crosses 85% on fills, evaluate lowering max_deployed_pct further (0.75 → 0.70) to enforce a firm top; (iii) any cluster (BFSI, Power, FMCG) crosses 25% of capital on fills, consider a cluster-specific override; (iv) realized daily P&L turns negative by > 1.5% of capital, evaluate tightening daily_loss_limit_pct from 0.03 → 0.025.
